text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

tr.td: 1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Albuterol sulfate 417 microgram/mL solution for inhalation
2.5 mg (0.6 mL of 417 microgram/mL solution) via nebulizer every 4 to 6 hours as needed
Acetaminophen 300 mg and hydrocodone bitartrate 5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (300 mg/5 mg) by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain
2 sprays (50 micrograms per spray) in each nostril once daily
Donepezil (as donepezil hydrochloride) 5 mg and memantine (as memantine hydrochloride) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms (1 spray) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Vitamin B12 and/or vitamin B12 derivative-containing product
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Development of individualized plan of care
Depression screening using PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire 9) score

ul.li: Patient will maintain orientation to person, place, and time as much as possible.
Patient will retain independence in basic activities of daily living such as bathing, dressing, and feeding.
Reduce frequency and severity of agitation, aggression, or other behavioral symptoms.
Patient or caregiver will administer dementia medications as prescribed.
Caregiver will receive education and support to manage patient’s dementia-related needs.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will adhere to recommended dietary, physical activity, and substance use modifications to support blood pressure control.
Patient will attend diabetes self-management education sessions to improve understanding of prediabetes and lifestyle modifications.
Maintain fasting plasma glucose within recommended range for prediabetes.
Maintain HbA1c below threshold for diabetes diagnosis.
Achieve and maintain weight loss as recommended for prediabetes management.
Patient will demonstrate understanding of asthma self-management techniques, including inhaler use, trigger avoidance, and symptom monitoring.
Patient will report no more than two episodes of asthma symptoms per week.
Patient will take prescribed asthma medications as directed, with at least 90% adherence.
Patient will monitor and record peak expiratory flow rate daily.

tr.td: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

ul.li: Patient will maintain orientation to person, place, and time as much as possible.
Patient will retain independence in basic activities of daily living such as bathing, dressing, and feeding.
Reduce frequency and severity of agitation, aggression, or other behavioral symptoms.
Patient or caregiver will administer dementia medications as prescribed.
Caregiver will receive education and support to manage patient’s dementia-related needs.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will adhere to recommended dietary, physical activity, and substance use modifications to support blood pressure control.
Patient will attend diabetes self-management education sessions to improve understanding of prediabetes and lifestyle modifications.
Maintain fasting plasma glucose within recommended range for prediabetes.
Maintain HbA1c below threshold for diabetes diagnosis.
Achieve and maintain weight loss as recommended for prediabetes management.
Patient will demonstrate understanding of asthma self-management techniques, including inhaler use, trigger avoidance, and symptom monitoring.
Patient will report no more than two episodes of asthma symptoms per week.
Patient will take prescribed asthma medications as directed, with at least 90% adherence.
Patient will monitor and record peak expiratory flow rate daily.
